preview_text:
The model favors Mexico to win at 70.5%, well ahead of Czech Republic's 6.7% chance. The gap reflects a substantial asymmetry in the forecast. The underdog is assigned a low probability of victory, leaving draws and Mexico success as the dominant outcomes.

The win-draw-loss spread shows a decisive lean toward a Mexico result. Draws carry a modest 22.8% likelihood, indicating the model sees this as fairly lopsided rather than tightly competitive. A Czech Republic upset remains in the tail of the distribution. The match is not expected to be closely matched.

A material divergence separates the model and sportsbook consensus. The model assigns Mexico 18 percentage points higher win probability than the market, while bookmakers give Czech Republic 14 points more chance than the statistical forecast. Polymarket pricing aligns closely with the sportsbook consensus. The dashboard does not claim which assessment is correct; the gap suggests meaningful disagreement between the two sources.
